gate_rows.name: request envelope builder
required_receipts: monitor accepted request image or terminal rejection
forbidden_fallbacks: Linux-built envelope as canonical monitor authority
Linux mutable request fields as post-copy authority
gate_rows.patch_precondition: monitor request schema and bounded-copy semantics modeled and source-anchored

gate_rows.name: direct-call wrapper and arch backend
required_receipts: terminal failure code or response handle
forbidden_fallbacks: wrapper return code as monitor approval
gate_rows.patch_precondition: arch/backend contract modeled for at least one target architecture and source-anchored as inert or no-code candidate

gate_rows.name: schema negotiation query path
required_receipts: monitor schema acceptance or terminal incompatibility
forbidden_fallbacks: Linux-visible schema support as acceptance authority
gate_rows.patch_precondition: schema compatibility model mapped to the concrete direct-call request/response field set

gate_rows.name: response-handle shadow refresh
forbidden_fallbacks: cached Linux shadow as monitor response
gate_rows.patch_precondition: response handle lifetime and revoke ordering modeled with stale-shadow counterexamples

gate_rows.name: control revoke lane
forbidden_fallbacks: Linux revoke state as monitor completion
gate_rows.patch_precondition: revoke ordering modeled with in-flight request, stale response, and retry cases

side_gates.name: test-only failure injection
side_gates.rule: must be test/KUnit-style only and cannot affect live decisions

side_gates.name: trace-only observation
side_gates.rule: requires separate tracefs runbook; no runtime coverage or public tracepoint ABI claim

required_before_patch: machine-readable gate row for each DCGATE item
source-map anchors for proposed Linux-facing surfaces or explicit future gaps
monitor responsibility statement for every receipt, epoch, replay, schema, response, and revoke field
failure-mode table for reject, retry, timeout, revoke, stale response, schema mismatch, and replay collision
validation plan separating source-only, compile-only, trace-only, and monitor-backed evidence
explicit non-claims for ABI, runtime coverage, monitor verification, and production protection
